packet_markdown:
# Columbia Square Living building packet

## Snapshot
- Building ID: columbia-square-living-1550-n-el-centro-ave
- Address: 1550 N El Centro Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90028
- Primary URL: https://www.columbiasquareliving.com/
- Management company: Greystar
- Recommended status: tour_candidate
- Last updated: 2026-03-22

## Scorecard
- Quiet: 3 (medium, evidence count 2)
- Management: 4 (medium, evidence count 2)
- Amenity: 5 (high, evidence count 2)
- Community stability: 4 (medium, evidence count 2)
- Location fit: 5
- Pricing transparency: 4

## Risks
- Structural noise risk: medium
- Location noise risk: medium
- Security risk: low
- Pricing risk: medium

## Evidence summary
- [official] The official site still markets Columbia Square Living as a modern high-amenity apartment community in Hollywood. Source: Columbia Square Living official site (https://www.columbiasquareliving.com/). Note: Official site describes a modern apartment community with world-class amenities.
- [review] Apartments.com currently shows a strong 4.8/5 rating across 13 reviews. Source: Apartments.com (https://www.apartments.com/columbia-square-living-los-angeles-ca/2v16sgc/). Note: Apartments.com showed 4.8 stars from 13 reviews on March 22, 2026.
- [review] Residents organized a public petition in 2024 asking management to reinstate valet service, indicating a real parking-operations friction point. Source: Change.org resident petition (https://www.change.org/p/reinstate-valet-service-at-columbia-square-living). Note: The petition says valet removal undermined a service residents considered part of the building’s value.

## Open questions
- Confirm whether the target unit faces away from adjacent construction and verify actual package handling, parking assignment, and approval speed. Confirm current parking operations, package handling, and any construction-exposed stacks.

## Notes
Tour candidate remains justified. Current public review signals are strong, but the resident valet petition and the construction-adjacency note mean parking and exposure still need direct verification before lease-level commitment.
